autumn/pk-lead-intelligence.reason: Salesforce CRM lead enrichment skill. `--allow-live` and `--dry-run` gate Salesforce Note writes to a CRM record, not orders on an exchange. The runtime has no exchange, no inventory, no order book, no money. The lexical heuristic flags the skill because it shares the `live_mode` / `dry_run` runtime pattern with sibling trading skills, but the trading-domain guardrails (`Trade Execution Contract`, `Pre-Trade Checklist`, marketable-exit rules, etc.) have nothing to validate here. Issue #753 documents the misclassification; the override is the minimum-change unblock for PR #752.

family-office/wire-beneficiary-verificat.reason: Issue #852 family-office operator skill. The runtime uses `--allow-live`, `live_mode`, and `dry_run` for review-gated operational workflows, not broker, exchange, order-book, CLOB, or inventory execution. Money movement, government filings, beneficiary changes, and binding instructions remain human-approved; the trading-domain safety contract is not applicable.

prophet/prophet-polymarket-edge.reason: Read-only at v1 by design (SKILL.md launch contract; design doc §13.10 / §15). `--yes-live` always exits non-zero. The runtime never places orders, holds inventory, or interacts with the CLOB. `scripts/trading_safety.py` exists ONLY to encode the three preconditions any future trading-enable PR must satisfy — its docstrings mirror trading-domain language from sibling Polymarket skills, which causes the pattern-based detector to classify this skill as trading. The order-book / marketability / pre-trade checklist rules apply only when execution is wired; at v1 they have nothing to validate. Issue #454 tracks the gate contract; revisit waivers when a future PR re-enables execution.
